findings.description: yaml.load() is called without an explicit SafeLoader, enabling arbitrary object construction/code execution (CWE-502) on PyYAML versions where the default or Full loader is unsafe; should be yaml.safe_load().
On PyYAML >= 6.0 yaml.load() requires a Loader argument, so this call raises TypeError and every load with an existing config file crashes on modern PyYAML.
Merge order is inverted relative to the documented precedence: the file overlay is applied after the env overlay, so file values silently override environment variables instead of env winning.
get()/require()/feature_enabled() call load() on every access, re-reading and re-parsing the YAML file, rescanning os.environ, and emitting an INFO log each time with no caching.
dict(DEFAULTS) is a shallow copy, so the nested feature_flags dict in the returned config aliases module-level DEFAULTS and caller mutation corrupts defaults for all subsequent loads.
If feature_flags is overridden to a non-dict (e.g., APP_FEATURE_FLAGS coerced to bool/str by _coerce, or a scalar in the file), flags.get raises AttributeError instead of returning False.
Malformed YAML raises an unhandled yaml.YAMLError, inconsistent with the loader's graceful degradation for missing files and non-mapping roots.

Eager coercion converts any numeric- or boolean-looking env string (numeric passwords, version strings like '1.0', 'inf'/'nan') to int/float/bool with no way to force a literal string, silently changing value types.
require() uses 'is None' to detect absence, so a key explicitly set to null in the config file is misreported as a missing required key.

Every APP_-prefixed env var is injected as a config key with no validation against known keys, so typos (e.g., APP_WORKERS) silently create new keys instead of overriding, and a bare 'APP_' var creates an empty-string key.
The os.path.exists guard races with open() (TOCTOU) and also passes for directories, so deletion in between or a directory path still raises an unhandled FileNotFoundError/IsADirectoryError.
An empty YAML file parses to None and is logged at ERROR as an invalid mapping even though an empty config file is a legitimate state.
The success log claims config was 'loaded from' the file path even when the file was missing or rejected, which is misleading during debugging.
File-provided values are returned without any type or schema validation, so e.g. worker_count set to a string in YAML silently replaces the integer default.
Optional[Any] is redundant since Any already includes None.
ConfigLoader and several public methods (require, feature_enabled) lack docstrings.
